S1: Introduction to Physics: Science in everyday life, laboratory apparatus, safety rules, and the scientific method
Measurements in Physics: Estimating and measuring length, area, volume, mass, density, and time using standard units
States of Matter: Kinetic theory of matter, arrangement of particles, physical changes, and diffusion
Forces and Their Effects: Types of forces, vector quantities, stretching materials (Hooke's Law), and friction
The Nature of Light: Sources of light, ray diagrams, shadows, eclipses, and reflection at plane surfaces
Temperature: Concept of hotness and coldness, mercury/alcohol thermometers, and temperature scales
Expansion of Solids, Liquids, and Gases: Thermal expansion, bimetallic strips, and the anomalous expansion of water
PRACTICAL S1: Fundamental Measurements, Mass and Density, Forces and Elasticity, Nature of Light Optics, Temperature & Expansion
S2: Work, Energy, and Power: Kinetic and potential energy, work calculations, conservation of energy, and power ratings
Simple Machines: Levers, pulleys, inclined planes, and gears (mechanical advantage, velocity ratio, and efficiency)
Pressure: Fluid pressure, atmospheric pressure, barometers, hydraulic systems, and transmission of pressure in fluids
Turning Effects of Forces (Moments): Principle of moments, center of gravity, and stability of structures
Curved Mirrors: Reflection at spherical surfaces, concave and convex mirrors, and real versus virtual images
Refraction of Light: Bending of light, refractive index, total internal reflection, Snell's law, and mirages
PRACTICAL S2: Work Energy Power, Simple Machines Efficiency MA VR, Fluid Pressure, Principle of Moments, Curved Mirrors, Refraction of Light
S3: Linear Motion: Speed, velocity, acceleration, distance-time graphs, and the equations of uniform motion
Newton's Laws of Motion: Inertia, momentum, action-reaction pairs, and safety applications like seatbelts
Heat Transfer: Mechanisms of conduction, convection, radiation, and applications like vacuum flasks
Electrostatics: Static electricity, electric charges, gold-leaf electroscopes, charging by induction, and lightning conductors
Current Electricity: Simple circuits, conductors, insulators, measuring current/voltage (ammeters/voltmeters), and Ohm's Law
Waves: Production and propagation of mechanical waves, transverse and longitudinal waves, and wave equations
Sound Waves: Characteristics of sound (pitch, loudness, quality), speed of sound, echoes, and ultrasound
PRACTICAL S3: Linear Motion Dynamics, Newton's Laws, Heat Transfer Mechanics, Electrostatics, Current Electricity, Waves, Sound Waves
S4: Magnetism: Properties of magnets, magnetic fields, magnetic induction, and the Earth's magnetic field
Magnetic Effect of an Electric Current: Electromagnets, force on a current-carrying conductor, electric motors, and moving-coil instruments
Electromagnetic Induction: Faraday's Law, Lenz's Law, alternating current (AC) generators, and step-up/step-down transformers
Mains Electricity: Electrical energy consumption calculation, wiring a three-pin plug, fuses, circuit breakers, and household safety
Modern Physics & Radioactivity: Atomic structure, alpha, beta, and gamma emissions, half-life, and safe handling of radioactive materials
Electronic Devices & Digital Electronics: Cathode ray oscilloscope (CRO), diodes, rectification, logic gates, and basic automation components
Earth and Space Physics: The solar system, life cycle of stars, galaxies, and satellite communications
PRACTICAL S4: Magnetic Fields, Electromagnetic Induction, Household Electricity, Electronics & Automation
